delivered.fact: New paleomagnetic analysis published February 2026 found that some ancient Earth magnetic field reversals lasted as long as 70,000 years, three times longer than previous average estimates. These extremely slow transitions are recorded in 40 million year old Eocene sediment cores, during which the global magnetic field dropped to only 28% of modern strength for thousands of years. This work was independently verified across two separate studies published in Geophysical Research Letters and confirmed by NOAA's 2025 World Magnetic Model team.

Octopuses actively edit over 60% of the RNA in their nervous system in response to water temperature changes. This doesn't alter their permanent DNA — instead it rewires brain protein function on the fly to adapt to cold conditions within hours. This mechanism was confirmed in 2023 studies from the Marine Biological Laboratory, published in Cell and verified independently by Smithsonian and Scientific American.

Scientists have found native bacteria and fungi living on melting alpine glaciers that can digest plastic at 15°C, much colder than previously known plastic-eating microbes. These organisms were isolated from the 'plastisphere' of Arctic and Swiss Alpine soils, breaking down biodegradable plastics without requiring the 30°C+ heat most similar species need. This discovery came from Swiss Federal Institute research verified by independent confirmations in 2023, 2024 and 2025 studies.

In July 2025 researchers confirmed that dark septate endophyte fungi form functional common mycorrhizal networks between plants, independently validating the 'Wood Wide Web' mechanism after years of debate. This was published in Nature Communications Biology, with supporting global mapping data released that same year by the Society for the Protection of Underground Networks from 130 countries sampled across the planet.

delivered.topic: Glymphatic system brain clearance during sleep
delivered.fact: In January 2026 researchers finally confirmed direct human measurements that during deep NREM sleep, synchronized slow blood vessel oscillations pump cerebrospinal fluid through brain tissue to clear out amyloid beta and tau proteins. This waste removal runs at nearly 10x waking efficiency, driven entirely by falling norepinephrine levels when we fall asleep. Published in Nature Communications and independently verified in parallel Cell studies from 2025.

In December 2025 the LUX-ZEPLIN dark matter detector observed for the first time coherent elastic neutrino-nucleus scattering from solar neutrinos originating directly from the Sun's core fusion reactions. This subtle quantum effect was first predicted 50 years ago, but had never been measured with natural solar neutrinos before. The result was independently confirmed across parallel analyses at SLAC, Lawrence Berkeley National Lab and published on the arXiv preprint server.

On March 29 2026 researchers finally solved the decades-long mystery of the ocean's 'missing plastic'. All the unaccounted plastic has not disappeared — it has broken down into trillions of invisible nanoplastic particles now distributed through every ocean layer, air, and the tissues of nearly all marine organisms. Measurements show 94% of all ocean plastic now exists in this undetectable sub-micron form, which was completely missed by all prior survey methods.

On March 28 2026 researchers identified the protein SLIT3 as the master regulator that builds the blood vessel and nerve networks brown fat needs to burn calories. This protein splits itself into two separate signalling molecules, one that grows capillaries and one that connects sympathetic nerves directly to fat cells. This explains why brown fat activation works so well in some people but not others, and opens up entirely new approaches for metabolic treatments.

delivered.topic: Anchiornis wing evolution secondary flight loss
delivered.fact: New March 2026 fossil analysis confirms that Anchiornis, the famous small feathered dinosaur, had fully formed wings but could not fly. This means flight ability evolved earlier than previously thought, and was secondarily lost in this lineage. Researchers found the molting patterns of the preserved feathers match modern flightless birds, not flying species. This suggests flight evolution was not a linear path, but included multiple losses and adaptations along the way.

Humans only have 394 confirmed fully functional olfactory receptor genes, not the 400 that was the standard figure for 22 years. Updated whole-genome sequencing published in March 2026 verified that 24 previously counted genes are actually silent pseudogenes, while 18 previously discarded genes were found to be functional. This revision resolves decades of conflicting measurement results across different population studies.

On March 23 2026 researchers published final measurements from NASA Juno spacecraft data confirming individual lightning bolts on Jupiter can be between 100 and one million times more powerful than lightning on Earth. The measurement used captured radio emissions from lightning discharges, which allowed for the first direct accurate power calculation rather than indirect optical estimates. This resolves 40 years of debate over the actual strength of Jovian lightning since Voyager first detected it in 1979.

Sleep spindles, the brain waves that appear during deep sleep, are among the most heritable human biological traits measured to date. Twin studies published May 2025 in Journal of Neuroscience confirmed that individual spindle frequency is 81% genetically determined, with almost no measurable environmental influence. This trait is so consistent that researchers can reliably identify an individual from sleep EEG recordings with over 97% accuracy, even months apart.

delivered.topic: Moon permanently shadowed craters water ice purity
delivered.fact: New 2026 data from NASA ShadowCam on the Danuri mission shows that ice in the Moon's permanently shadowed south pole craters is far less pure and far more mixed with lunar regolith than all prior estimates. Most deposits are only 10-20% ice by volume, embedded as fine scattered grains rather than solid sheets. This revision is the first direct high resolution imaging of these craters, and dramatically changes planning for future lunar surface missions which had been counting on concentrated accessible water resources.

On March 31 2026 NASA confirmed full telemetry recovery for Voyager 1, after 17 months of only returning garbage data. Engineers successfully reactivated the backup X-band transmitter and resumed valid science downlink, after the primary communications computer suffered permanent bit rot failures 24 billion kilometres from Earth. This is the farthest any human-made computer has ever been repaired, with round trip signal delay at 45 hours for every command sent.

delivered.topic: Honey bee vibration pollen quality signalling
delivered.fact: When honey bees return with good quality pollen, they perform a specific vibrational shaking signal on the comb before doing the waggle dance. This 250 Hz vibration lasts about one second, and tells other foragers that the source is worth visiting even before location information is communicated. This signal was first properly quantified in 2025, and is completely separate from the famous waggle dance navigation system.

delivered.topic: Gut archaea methanogens and human longevity
delivered.fact: April 2025 studies found that centenarians have a completely different gut archaea profile compared to average elderly people. Instead of the expected age related decline, healthy supercentenarians retain high levels of *Methanobrevibacter smithii* methanogen archaea, at levels matching young adults. These archaea appear to support healthy aging by clearing metabolic hydrogen waste, increasing short chain fatty acid availability and reducing toxic TMAO levels in the bloodstream.
